name: Demon of Pride
description: The spirit of spiritual self-delusion. It twists your strengths into vanity, making virtue indistinguishable from pride.

name: Demon of Despair
description: The noonday demon. It drains will and meaning from every act, making the path ahead feel pointless.

name: Demon of Vainglory
description: The hunger for empty praise. It corrupts virtue into performance, making the audience more real than God.

name: Demon of Anger
description: Righteous wrath unchecked. It burns everything it passes through — friend and foe alike.

name: Demon of Lust
description: Disordered desire wearing the face of comfort. It confuses love with consumption, warmth with possession.

name: Archon of Pride
description: A greater prelest — ancient and subtle. It has perfected the art of appearing as divine revelation while exalting the self.
intent: Reveals a false vision of greatness
Whispers that you are already holy

name: Lord of Sloth
description: The deep form of acedia — not laziness but a total withdrawal from being. It has made peace with the void.
intent: Suggests that love itself is delusion

name: Prince of Vanity
description: The prince of all performance. It has lived so long among those who pray to be seen that it has become indistinguishable from piety.
intent: Mirrors your best qualities back as pride
Offers fame as a form of holiness
Makes discernment itself feel like vanity

name: Duke of Wrath
description: Ancient wrath that has become a structure. It no longer requires a cause — the burning is its own justification now.
intent: Strikes with the force of accumulated grievance

name: Seraph of Corruption
description: A demonic mockery of the seraphim. It offers a counterfeit of divine union — intimacy without the Cross, fullness without kenosis.
intent: Offers ecstasy as a substitute for theosis
Corrupts every longing for God into something lesser
